title: pandanaceae
intro: The family rank Pandanaceae is a group of flowering plants that includes around 750 species, primarily found in tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a family of monocots, characterized by their unique inflorescence and often, highly fragrant leaves. The Pandanaceae family is commonly known for its economically important species, such as the screwpine (Pandanus spp.), which provides food, shelter, and other essential resources for local communities. The diverse range of species within this family plays a vital role in maintaining the ecological balance of their native habitats.
